questions.question: What are the voltage and amperage ratings of each alternator on the DA42?

options.text: 14 volts DC, 60 amps
28 volts DC, 70 amps
24 volts DC, 50 amps
28 volts DC, 100 amps

questions.explanation: The DA42 has two belt-driven, 28-volt DC, 70-amp alternators — one on each engine.

questions.question: Can the ECU buses receive power from an alternator that has its switch in the OFF position?

options.text: No, the alternator switch must be ON for any bus to receive power
Yes, the ECU buses can draw power directly from the alternator regardless of switch position
Only if the main battery is fully charged
Only during an emergency declared by the pilot

questions.explanation: The Left and Right ECU Buses have a special design feature: they can take power directly from the alternator even if the alternator switch is in the OFF position, providing extra redundancy for engine management.

questions.question: Where is the main battery located on the DA42, and what type is it?

options.text: A lithium-ion battery in the tail cone
A 24-volt, 13.6 amp-hour lead acid battery in the center-aft side of the front baggage compartment
A nickel-cadmium battery behind the instrument panel
A 28-volt lead acid battery under the rear passenger seats

questions.explanation: The main battery is a 24-volt, 13.6 amp-hour lead acid battery located in the center-aft side of the front baggage compartment.

questions.question: How long can the ECU backup batteries power the ECUs during a total electrical failure?

options.text: 15 minutes
60 minutes
30 minutes
2 hours

questions.explanation: The ECU backup batteries (four lead-acid batteries under the rear passenger seats) provide 30 minutes of power to ONLY the ECUs during a total electrical failure.

questions.question: Which alternator control unit is responsible for balancing the load between the two alternators?

options.text: The Right Alternator Control Unit
Both units share the balancing function equally
The Left Alternator Control Unit
A separate dedicated load balancing module

questions.explanation: The Left Alternator Control Unit has a special role: its voltage regulator balances the load between the two alternators so they produce similar output.
